note: The glowing links below are an illustrative attention map — hand-drawn by the author to show which other words a model must connect to in order to pin down the meaning of the highlighted word. They are a teaching picture of how attention works, not weights pulled from one specific model's internals.
instructions: Click any underlined word. The words it must 'pay attention to' will light up — brighter means a stronger pull.

focus.caption: Here 'bank' leans hard on 'river' and 'current' — water words. Same five letters, but the meaning is built from its neighbors.
Now the very same word 'bank' lights up 'savings' and 'money' instead. The model never stored one meaning for 'bank' — it computes the meaning fresh from context every time.
'winter' and 'arrived' pull 'spring' toward the season. No calendar needed — the surrounding words settle it.
Swap the neighbors and 'spring' becomes a coil. 'mattress', 'rusty', and 'poked' do the disambiguating.

focus.meaning: harsh in tone (not a physical edge)
focus.caption: Nothing here is literally pointy. 'reply' and 'turned away' bend 'sharp' toward a feeling, not a blade.
